heading: What's New in 6.15
body:
The Node.js driver v6.15 release includes the following features:

- Adds support for supplying custom AWS credentials when configuring [authentication](node-authentication-mechanisms) or [In-Use Encryption](node-fle). You
  must supply the custom function signature in the following form:

  ```javascript
  () => Promise<AWS.Credentials>
  ```

  You can also specify provider chains from the AWS SDK, allowing you to
  customize your options. To view examples of how to provide custom
  credentials, see the [v6.15.0 Release Notes](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v6.15.0) on GitHub.

- Fixes a bug that caused a `MongoClient` configured for `OIDC` with an
  `AbortSignal` to abort on the cursor when the client reauthenticates.

- Fixes a bug that caused an unhandled rejection to throw when a `timeoutms`
  expires before an operation reaches the server selection process.

- Fixes a bug caused by closing a change stream while processing a change event.
  This bug caused a potential error to reject an internal promise representing
  the "next" change.

To learn more about this release, see the
[v6.15.0 Release Notes](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v6.15.0) on
GitHub.
